steps.title: Find the slant heights
steps.content:
The base area is
$$
18\cdot 9=162
$$
For the two faces with base $18$, the slant height uses half the width:
$$
\sqrt{4.5^2+12^2}=\sqrt{164.25}=\frac{3\sqrt{73}}{2}
$$
For the two faces with base $9$, the slant height uses half the length:
$$
\sqrt{9^2+12^2}=15
$$

steps.title: Add the face areas
steps.content:
The two triangular faces with base $18$ have total area
$$
2\cdot \frac{1}{2}\cdot 18\cdot \frac{3\sqrt{73}}{2}=27\sqrt{73}
$$
The two triangular faces with base $9$ have total area
$$
2\cdot \frac{1}{2}\cdot 9\cdot 15=135
$$

steps.title: Compute total surface area
steps.content: Add the base area and triangular face areas: $162+135+27\sqrt{73}=297+27\sqrt{73}$. This is about $527.7$. The answer is 527.7.
